details: The package's index.js is heavily obfuscated using a string-array shuffler pattern (while(!![]) loop with repeated ['shift']() calls and large hexadecimal indices). Obfuscation of this kind is unusual for a legitimate npm library and frequently appears in supply-chain attacks to hide network exfiltration, credential reads, or remote payload execution. However, the obfuscated logic was not deobfuscated and traced in this run, so the actual runtime behavior — whether it performs exfiltration, fetches remote code, or merely hides benign logic — has not been confirmed. The package name also suggests possible confusion with the well-known `express` ecosystem, which warrants additional scrutiny by a human reviewer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
